methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Sunday Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Xiongyi Cui and Linxi Fan. The valuation universe comprises one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics among the top private comparables.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. While 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, the dataset contains zero same-stage datapoints, zero adjacent-stage datapoints, and zero similar-tier comparables. This falls below the minimum threshold of three datapoints required for a reliable peer-median approach. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity of valuation stages and company profiles within the broader robotics landscape.

Given the absence of sufficient narrow-stage peer data, traditional triangulation cannot be completed at this time. A credible valuation would require either: expansion of the private comparable set to identify additional seed-stage robotics companies, or application of alternative methods such as venture capital method or revenue-multiple analysis if forward-looking financial projections are available. The single public comparable provides limited guidance for a pre-revenue or early-revenue seed company. Further diligence is necessary before a defensible valuation range can be established.
